title: Data Ingestion
description: The Data Ingestion module enables research teams to upload, map, and transform source data into the OMOP Common Data Model. Use this workflow to bring external datasets into Parthenon by uploading raw files, defining schema mappings to align source fields with OMOP domains, and resolving concept mappings to standardize vocabularies before data becomes available for cohort building and analysis.
tips: Begin with the Upload step to validate file format and row counts before proceeding to schema mapping, catching structural issues early.
Use the schema mapping editor to preview how source columns align to OMOP domains; unmapped fields are highlighted in amber for quick identification.
Concept mapping can be seeded automatically from the platform vocabulary; review auto-suggestions carefully and manually resolve any low-confidence matches.
Run a dry-run ingestion to generate a validation report without committing data, allowing QA review before final load.
Check the Jobs panel after submitting an ingestion run to monitor progress and download detailed error logs if any records fail to load.
